conventions.A1: one attack event per class-stack per battle turn
conventions.cadence_floor: fires at k,2k,..: n=floor(T/k) (primary; Martin-confirmed report_001 notes; Vulcanus S3 triangulated 2026-07-18)

ratio_tests.note: T-free conditional binomial; assumes both skills share one opportunity stream

pvp_t12_report_001.json.calibration_finding_2026_07_05: CORRECTED (Martin caught the error). My first reconstruction built units from PANELS ONLY - it applied the T12 troop skills but NOT the hero skills - and compared against r6/r8 which DO have their hero skills (via assemble). Invalid comparison. Also r6/r8 are a PRE-T12 regime (no T12 troop skills, sub-Gen11 heroes) so cannot be force-fit with this T12 regime (T12 skills + Gen14 heroes) under one rate/def_k/def_ed. REDONE via construct.build (hero skills applied): skills add +8% stat (skillmult x1.08) + LARGE Damage-Dealt (+55% inf/marks, +100% lancer). That ~1.5-2x extra damage crushes the battle to ~8 turns (real ~30+) and inflates the attacker win to ~70% survivors (real 3.45%) - the rate=168 calibrated on r6/r8's lower-DD PRE-T12 config does NOT fit this high-DD T12 config. So my earlier 'definitive structural mis-ranking' claim is RETRACTED - with skills in, the dominant issue is DD-driven over-acceleration + a regime/duration mismatch, and one T12 point cannot cleanly separate structure from calibration. Possible over-count in DD (conditional/targeted skills applied as flat - assemble v0 approximation) - TBD. PATH: calibrate the T12 regime SEPARATELY (own rate/def_k/def_ed), which needs SEVERAL T12 near-even reports (esp. an attacker-barely-LOSES) + this battle's real round count (Bradley S3 counter). Until then near-even T12 = untrustworthy (calibrated=false).
pvp_t12_report_001.json.reconstructed_timeline_2026_07_05: Martin reconstructed the clock from hero-skill trigger counts. REVISED to 16 turns (was 15): the clean anchor is Vulcanus SK3 '+60% def, every 3 turns' triggered 6x -> turns 1,4,7,10,13,16 -> marksman alive at T16 -> battle=16. Revised Vulcanus SK2 reading: 'every 5 attacks PER TROOP TYPE' (not total-side): his inf floor(12/5)=2 + lancer floor(14/5)=2 + marksman floor(16/5)=3 = 7 triggers. Other triggers: his Elif SK3 x12 (infantry die endT12), enemy Elif SK3 x15 (enemy infantry die endT15), enemy Cara x6 every-2 (enemy marksman die endT12). STAGING: T12 his INF die + enemy MARKSMAN die (his ambush); his LANCER die endT14 (revised from T12); T13-16 his MARKSMAN carry; enemy INF die endT15, enemy LANCER die endT16 (LAST to fall - their counter=his infantry died T12); his 62,364 marksman survive. MECHANICS (Martin, authoritative): volleys are STRICT FRONT-TO-BACK (inf->lancer->marksman); marksman are IMMORTAL behind a live wall EXCEPT bypass procs = enemy lancer Ambusher procs + enemy Cara SK3 (periodic every-2 burst hitting lancer+marksman directly). So his marksman were only chipped ~690k->~580k over T1-12 by procs, held FLAT ~580k T13-T15start (lancers soaked), exposed only T15-16. ENDGAME explains the razor-thin result: T15 ~0/0/580k vs enemy ~1/348k/0; his exposed marksman grind 348k enemy lancers over T15-16 at a COUNTER DISADVANTAGE (lancer counters marksman), losing ~518k to kill ~348k, ending 62,364 - won on NUMBERS alone (580k>348k); ~10% fewer marks = loss. NEAR-MUTUAL-ANNIHILATION, attacker edged 3.45%. Casualty check EXACT: 783589+331529+(690154-62364)=1,742,908=incap. Paired with report_002 (25t, LANCERS survive) this is a controlled near-even experiment - see report_002 engine_finding for the TWO structural gaps (casualty-allocation order + near-balance hypersensitivity). Both are validation anchors; neither is reproducible by the current engine.
pvp_t12_report_001.json.specials_note: Both sides +20% troops A/D/L/H items; -20% enemy A/D; pets +10% A/D/L/H both; enemy pet penalties Def -10% / Leth -5% / HP -5% both. Attacker widgets: Rally Attack +15%, Rally Lethality +15%. Defender widgets: Defender Defense +15%, Defender Lethality +15%. All already baked into panel_pct.
pvp_t12_report_001.json.stats_note: panel_pct = the in-game Stat Bonuses panel (FULL SCOUTED - includes heroes, gear, items, pets, widgets, penalties). Since the two sides differ, this is the ACTUAL-stats case (NOT pre-assumed symmetric) - feed as-is, NO hero relayering. KEY ASYMMETRY: attacker is OFFENSE-focused (higher Attack/Lethality), defender is DEFENSE-focused (higher Defense/Health) - via the widgets (attacker Rally Attack/Leth +15%, defender Defender Def/Leth +15%).
pvp_t12_report_001.json.t12_notes: Attacker HIGHER T12 research (Meridian 5 vs 3, Starfire 8 vs 3 -> +5%/+4% marks buffs vs +3%/+1.5%). Indomitable Wall tied L4 (-2.4% enemy dmg both). Likely decisive in the near-even outcome.

pvp_t12_report_002.json.engine_finding_2026_07_05: CORRECTED (Martin: absorption IS strict front-to-back - the ORDER mechanic is RIGHT, not a bug). The real gap: the engine models hero skills as AVERAGED modifiers (Vulcanus SK2 +100%dd/freq5 -> ~+20% avg; Cara SK3 +40%dd/freq2 -> ~+20% avg) and does NOT fire discrete procs, so it cannot reproduce BACKLINE ANNIHILATION VIA BYPASS PROCS. report_002: his MARKSMAN (626k) were annihilated by ~T14 by enemy Ambusher procs + enemy Cara SK3 bursts (both bypass the front, hit marksman directly), while his LANCERS stayed immortal behind infantry until T19 then survived (118,068). The engine's only bypass is a fixed 20% ambush fraction (LANCER_BYPASS_CHANCE) - too weak - and Cara SK3 is not modeled as a backline hit, so the engine keeps marksman alive in BOTH battles (always returns MARKSMAN survivor). TWO real gaps: (1) skills must FIRE discretely turn-by-turn (with correct bypass magnitudes) to reproduce NON-LINEAR proc-driven attrition + the correct survivor type; averaging cannot. (2) near-balance hypersensitivity/bimodality (exact survivor% is chaotic near even). PATH = re-architect to a turn-by-turn skill-firing simulator (which also emits the trigger+kill telemetry the front-end now wants). Both battles are the validation anchors.
pvp_t12_report_002.json.reconstructed_timeline_2026_07_05: Martin reconstructed from hero-skill trigger counts. ROBUST anchors: (1) battle = 25 turns [Vulcanus SK3 'every 3 turns' triggered 9x -> turns 1,4,7,...,25 -> (9-1)*3+1=25]. (2) his infantry died end T19 [his Elif SK3 = 19]. (3) enemy infantry died end T22 [enemy Elif SK3 = 22]. (4) enemy marksman died ~T18 [enemy Cara 'every 2' = 9x -> 9*2=18]. (5) his LANCERS are the sole survivors (118,068). WRINKLE: Vulcanus SK2 (revised to 'every 5 attacks PER TROOP TYPE') = 10 triggers implies his MARKSMAN died ~T10-14 [inf floor(19/5)=3 + lancer floor(25/5)=5 + marks=2 -> 3+5+2=10], i.e. his marksman died EARLY. RESOLVED (Martin): his MARKSMAN were annihilated by ~T14 by enemy lancer Ambusher procs + enemy Cara SK3 bursts (bypass the front, hit marksman directly), NON-LINEARLY (step drops at each proc), while his LANCERS stayed IMMORTAL behind infantry until infantry died T19, then survived to T25 (118,068). So death order his side: MARKSMAN (~T14, via bypass procs) -> INFANTRY (T19, front wall) -> LANCER survives. permanent LOSSES=33,199 (non-zero here vs 0 in report_001).
pvp_t12_report_002.json.stats_note: panel_pct IDENTICAL to report_001 (same players/setup minutes apart). FULL SCOUTED. Special bonuses identical to report_001 (attacker +20% troop A/D/L/H, -20% enemy A/D, Rally Atk+15%/Leth+15% widgets, pets +10% all & enemy pet penalties; defender same but Defender Def+15%/Leth+15% widgets). t12_skill_levels ASSUMED same as report_001 (not separately visible in this report - same player, minutes apart). Attacker OFFENSE-focused, defender DEFENSE-focused.

pvp_t12_report_003.json.skill_triggers_note: From the Skill Details panel (rows are icon-keyed; slot mapping partly uncertain): attacker Gisela x1/x19/x19/x59/x1, defender Gisela x1/x19/x18/x46. Flora (att lancer): x64/x1/x10/x5 + one row 6 triggers 4,759 kills. Karol (def lancer): 3 triggers 368 kills, x1. Ligeia (att marks): x19 w/ 7,328 kills, x19, x5, 6 triggers w/ 903 kills. Vulcanus (def marks): 16 triggers w/ 10,212 kills, x13, 7 triggers w/ 2,499 kills, x1. CLOCK: Gisela every-turn rows x19 both sides -> battle ~19-20 turns; defender Gisela x18 -> def infantry likely died end T18-19. Vulcanus 7-trigger row matches every-3-turns at ~19-20 turns (floor((19-1)/3)+1=7). OPEN PUZZLE: Vulcanus 16-trigger row exceeds the 'every 5 attacks per troop type' formula (~9-12 at this length) - cadence semantics still not fully solved; direct skill kills (both sides ~13k total) are small vs 217k casualties.
pvp_t12_report_003.json.specials_note: Both sides: +20% troops A/D/L/H items, -20% enemy A/D items, pets +10% A/D/L/H, enemy pet penalties -10% def/-5% leth/-5% hp. Defender widget: Defender Troops' Attack +15% (attacker has no attacker-side widget - solo attack). All baked into panel_pct (panel_is_final=true, widgets_in_panel=true).
pvp_t12_report_003.json.structural_ground_truth: GOLD per-class facts no rally anchor provides: (1) attacker LANCERS took ZERO casualties across ~19-20 turns -> the infantry wall NEVER fell (2,421 inf survived) -> strict front-to-back absorption held for the ENTIRE battle on the winning side. (2) attacker MARKSMAN lost 66.5% (45,375) while the wall stood -> ALL marksman damage arrived via BYPASS (defender Ambusher procs from 26,917 T11 lancers + any backline skills) -> bypass magnitude is large and sustained. (3) defender fully wiped in ~19-20 turns despite HIGHER panels (+3875 vs +3311 attack) -> count (+25.3%), tier (T12 vs T11 on lancer/marks), T12 stacks (3/1/2 vs 0/0/0), and composition geometry (25% T12 lancers ambushing a 45% marksman-heavy enemy; small 26.9k enemy lancer wall) DOMINATED the panel deficit.

pvp_t12_report_004.json.clock_note: Both Ligeias' every-2 skill triggered 8x -> ~15-17 turns; Flora row x15; Hervor x13; att Gisela x23 (cadence unclear - OPEN, do not force). Turns gate set [14,18].
pvp_t12_report_004.json.skill_kill_oracle: PER-SKILL KILLS from the report (both sides - the first clean skill-packet magnitude anchor): attacker Gisela 0 kills (buff rows only, triggers 1/9/8/23); Karol one row 6 triggers 3,267 kills; att Ligeia 8 triggers 6,965 kills (SK2-class) + 4 triggers 3,121 kills. Defender Hervor 0 kills (1/1/1/13); Flora 3 triggers 637 kills (rows x15/x1/x4/x4); def Ligeia 8 triggers 4,723 kills + 7 triggers 3,850 kills. TOTAL skill-attributed kills ~22.6k of 312k casualties = ~7%. ENGINE AT DIAGNOSIS TIME: def Ligeia SK2+SK3 = 99k kills (12x real) - skill packets over-scaled.
pvp_t12_report_004.json.specials_note: Both sides: +20% troops A/D/L/H items, -20% enemy A/D items, pets +10% A/D/L/H, enemy pet penalties. Defender widgets: Defender Troops' Defense +15%, Lethality +15%, Health +15% (attacker none - solo). All baked into panel_pct.
pvp_t12_report_004.json.structural_ground_truth: Same wall physics as anchor 3, now vs a marksman-heavy garrison: (1) attacker LANCERS again ZERO casualties (26,197 intact) - her wall held the whole battle (inf 157k->59k survivors). (2) Her MARKSMAN lost only 14% (bypass procs only - enemy lancers just 20k). (3) The defender's tiny 62k wall fell early, exposing 141k marksman which were then wiped - marksman-heavy comps collapse once the wall breaks. (4) DEFENDER PANELS LETH/HEALTH-STACKED (+36% leth, +36% health vs attacker) YET LOST DECISIVELY with a 1.29x count disadvantage - leth/health contribute far less than the engine's beast-fitted exponents (L linear, H^1.45) predict. This is the sharpest anchor for the damage-formula stat weighting.

pvp_t12_report_005.json.skill_kill_oracle: Per-skill kills: att Gregory 17 triggers 64,253 kills (+rows 1/1/42); att Mia 27 / 23 triggers 32,814 kills / 17; att Blanchette 1 / 8x29,491 / 12x95,815 / 3 / 3x11,967. Def Magnus 1/7/1/37 (0 kills); def Freya 1 / 15 triggers 96,582 kills / 1 / 7 / 6x24,293 / 3; def Blanchette 9x22,553 / 13x41,484 / 3 / 12x47,434. Total skill-attributed: att ~234k of 1.16M dealt (~20%), def ~232k of 1.42M dealt (~16%) - HIGHER share than A4's ~7% (older-gen heroes, longer battle).
pvp_t12_report_005.json.specials_note: Both sides: +20% troops A/D/L/H items, -20% enemy A/D items. NO pet buffs (older battle, 2025-07). Attacker widgets: Rally Attack +15%, Rally Lethality +15%. Defender widgets: Defender Defense +15%, Defender Health +15%. All in panel_pct.
pvp_t12_report_005.json.structural_ground_truth: (1) FIRST ATTACKER DEFEAT: near-mirror totals, defender won keeping 18.4% - pins the far side of the knife-edge and proves near-even rally-vs-garrison defenders fight at >= parity (the A1/A2 inference, now with a defender-win witness). (2) DUPLICATE JOINERS REAL DATA: with the CORRECTED comp this battle genuinely constrains duplicate stacking (see item 4); the earlier 'joiners are not the cause' finding applied to the mistranslated comp only. (3) CORRECTED COMP: attacker = inf 744,843 + marksman 676,341, NO lancers (both classes on strong ~+2647% panels). (4) FINAL STATE (flat lock def_k=0.45; the third-pass size-dependent scale was REVERTED as error-induced). The engine ranks the ATTACKER ahead regardless of joiners (0-joiner p_win 1.00, ~65% survivors; 4x-Nora-dedup ~68%) vs the real DEFEAT. The marksman correction made the attacker look STRONGER than the mistranslated lancers would (marksman +2647% panel vs lancer +1719%), so it WIDENED the sim/reality gap. The miss is therefore the BASE matchup, not Nora: a near-mirror battle (counts within 0.04%, panels within ~15-25%) the engine can't call - flagged coin_flip by the +-20% band. Duplicate-joiner DEDUP is implemented (same-hero joiner SK1 applies once; enemy Jessie x2 dedups symmetrically) as the more-correct mechanic, but it is NOT the driver of this defeat. ERROR-INDEPENDENT FINDING worth a clean future re-calibration: anchors 1/2/5 (all real rallies) suggest large multi-player garrison defenders are STRONGER than flat def_k=0.45 implies (A1/A2 deep 3-6% grinds, A5 defender win) - exactly what the reverted size-dependent scale was targeting; it should be re-derived on corrected data if pursued, NOT resurrected from the mistranslated cliff.
